summary: A permanently neutral, non-EU alpine state that hosts global institutions and is the centre of private banking, pharmaceutical giants, and international diplomacy — all while being surrounded by the EU.

history.detail: Three cantons form defensive alliance; considered Switzerland's founding.
Peace of Westphalia; Swiss independence from Holy Roman Empire confirmed.
Congress of Vienna recognises and guarantees Swiss neutrality.
Henri Dunant founds ICRC; Geneva becomes humanitarian capital.

history.event: League of Nations HQ in Geneva
history.detail: Switzerland becomes centre of international diplomacy.
Last major country to join; maintained neutrality by delaying.

finance.currency_role: CHF — safe-haven currency; negative interest rates used to prevent overvaluation

tech_startups.vc_ecosystem: Strong biotech and medtech; ETH spin-offs

resources.agriculture: Dairy, cheese, chocolate; protected domestic market

follow_the_money.detail: ~$2.4T in managed offshore wealth; historical secrecy eroded by FATCA/OECD
Central bank of central banks; coordinates global monetary policy in Basel
Annual global elite gathering; shapes international policy agenda

theories_disputed.detail: Volcker Committee 1998 confirmed Swiss banks held looted WWII Jewish assets; $1.25B settlement paid.

connections.detail: Largest trade partner; deeply integrated supply chains
Swiss banks manage major US wealth; FATCA compliance forced disclosure
